description: Doorway-like city coverage with little or no real local evidence
recommended_response: Map every location page to local proof: photos, projects, staff/service notes, or merge into stronger hubs.

description: Review volume or wording that appears disconnected from natural customer experience
recommended_response: Document neutral review practices and avoid incentives, pressure, or selective solicitation.

description: Generic AI copy that sounds confident but does not show field judgment
recommended_response: Attach repairability logic, inspection notes, code-aware planning, and project examples.

description: Claims such as best, top, trusted, local, certified, or insurance-ready with no proof trail
recommended_response: Create source-spine references, credentials pages, photo provenance, schema, and plain-English caveats.

description: Pages written only for keywords, not for answer extraction
recommended_response: Write definitions, comparisons, FAQs, and evidence summaries that can be cited without hype.

description: Roofing content blurs contractor documentation and public adjusting
recommended_response: Use Claim Verifiability language: document observable roof conditions; do not decide coverage.

description: Photos exist but have no labels, stage, defect, or project context
recommended_response: Use public-safe photo labels, galleries, and proof-route metadata.

description: Pages sell replacement without explaining standards, manufacturer specs, or local code context
recommended_response: Use Code to Spec Roofing language and explain why installation standards matter.
